core_mechanism: bounding the convergence (mixing) time of a reversible local-update Markov chain by lower-bounding its spectral gap and logarithmic-Sobolev constant, using these functional inequalities to certify exponential relaxation to the stationary distribution and to quantify how the chain's speed depends on imposed boundary values
brief_reason: The abstract's entire result is showing that the spectral gap and log-Sobolev constant of the local-update dynamics are bounded below by an n-independent constant, which is precisely what yields the O(log n) mixing-time bound and captures the boundary-condition sensitivity.
